senses.definition: a device or instrument used to measure or assess shape-related properties, such as curvature or form, in a specific context
examples: The engineer used a shapometer to evaluate the car body curvature.
Researchers developed a shapometer to quantify the smoothness of the sculpture's surface.

senses.definition: a common misspelling or informal pronunciation of 'shampoo', a hair cleansing product
examples: I bought a new shapoo to keep my hair shiny.
The label clearly says it's a gentle shapoo for daily use.

senses.definition: informal or dialect plural of 'shape' referring to forms or appearances; rare or nonstandard usage
examples: The clay takes on interesting shaps under the wheel.
We compared the shaps of the two sculptures.

senses.definition: a term that may refer to a chemical or fictional substance; not widely attested in standard dictionaries
examples: The lab catalog lists shaptan as a reactive compound.
In the novel, shaptan powers the alien device.

senses.definition: a term with limited or specialized usage, possibly a brand, chemical, or fictional element; not widely recognized
examples: The inventor mixed shaptin into the formulation.
Shaptin is described as a stabilizer in the manual.

senses.definition: informal spelling of 'shapey' or 'shapely' meaning having a pleasing or well-defined shape
examples: That dress has a shapy silhouette.
He admired the shapy curve of the bike frame.

senses.definition: able to be shared with others or easily shareable online or in social contexts
examples: The article is sharable on social media.
We prepared sharable packets for the conference.

senses.definition: a proper noun likely referring to a person, place, or organization; usage depends on context
examples: Sharada Institute released a new grant.
We met Sharada at the conference.

senses.definition: a name from Indian languages meaning 'blessing' or a short form of a devotional term; used as a given name or place name
examples: Sharan is a common given name in some regions.
The temple accepts offerings from Sharan patrons.

senses.definition: an aesthetic or cultural term referring to a specific style or movement featuring elaborate decoration; used primarily in historical or artistic contexts
examples: The gallery showcased Sharawadgi as a key influence in East Asian garden design.
Scholars debate the authenticity of Sharawadgi in classical landscapes.

senses.definition: plural form of sharawadgi, referring to multiple instances or discussions of the aesthetic concept
examples: The collection includes several Sharawadgis from East Asian gardens.
Academic papers compare various Sharawadgis.

senses.definition: variant spelling of sharawadgi; refers to the same concept in garden design and aesthetics
examples: The term sharawaggi appears in older texts on landscape art.
Sharawaggi-inspired motifs appear in the scroll paintings.

senses.definition: plural of sharawaggi, referring to multiple instances of the aesthetic concept
examples: The texts discuss various Sharawaggis across colonies.
Collectors seek Sharawaggis for their historical value.

senses.definition: a small piece or fragment of a hard material, such as glass or pottery, that has broken off
examples: I picked up a shard of broken glass from the floor.
The pot shattered into many shards.

senses.definition: to break into pieces with sharp edges; to crack or fragment
examples: The vase sharded when it fell.
The ice sharded under the weight of the bounce.

senses.definition: an ancient people known as the Sea Peoples, associated with the Philistines in historical records
examples: The inscriptions mention the Shardana as seafaring warriors.
The Shardana played a role in Late Bronze Age politics.

senses.definition: divided into pieces or segments, often in computing or materials contexts
examples: The database was sharded to improve performance.
Sharded glass creates a mosaic effect.

senses.definition: the process of dividing a database or data set into smaller, more manageable pieces called shards
examples: Sharding distributes load across servers.
We implemented sharding to scale the system.

senses.definition: multiple small pieces or fragments of a brittle material
examples: The mirror lay in shards on the floor.
She collected the shards of pottery for her project.

senses.definition: informal or informal descriptive term meaning covered in or resembling shards; sharp-edged
examples: The shardy surface cut my finger.
A shardy coastline can be dangerous to walk along.

senses.definition: to give a part of something to another or to have a portion of something with others
examples: I want to share this cake with you.
She shares her notes with the class.

senses.definition: a portion of something that is given to or taken by another person; part of a whole
examples: We each took a share of the money.
He received a share of the profits.

senses.definition: the capacity or quality of being shareable or capable of being shared
examples: The app emphasizes high shareabilities for users.
We evaluated the shareabilities of different content formats.

senses.definition: the quality of being easy to share, especially on social media
examples: The video has great shareability.
marketers look for high shareability in campaigns.

senses.definition: able to be shared; easy to share online or with others
examples: That meme is highly shareable.
We found a few shareable charts from the report.

senses.definition: a term that may refer to a bone or structural element involved in sharing or support; not common in standard usage
examples: The diagram shows the sharebone in the sculpture's framework.

senses.definition: a person or firm that buys and sells shares on behalf of clients; a stockbroker
examples: The sharebroker recommended a blue-chip stock.
She spoke with her sharebroker to adjust her portfolio.
